mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the Industrial Production index (INDPRO) indicate expanding real economic activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over a rolling 3-year window the Rank IC of INDPRO on 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03, or if positive INDPRO months are followed by negative returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises to or above 0.25%, it signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the 5 trading days following a FEDFUNDS >= 0.25% event the median SPX return is non-negative or if the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a coordinated expansion in both the supply side and labor market, reinforcing corporate revenue and profit growth, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling period the average 60-day forward return following months with both INDPRO and PAYEMS above their 6-month moving averages is less than the unconditional average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY growth below 3%), a moderate rise in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals strengthening demand without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, thus supporting equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods with CPI YoY below 3%, the correlation between CPIAUCSL month-over-month change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in Retail and Food Services Sales (RSAFS) above 5% indicates a consumption surge that boosts corporate revenues and short-term equity returns, as investors price in improved earnings prospects over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the 5 trading days after a RSAFS MoM >5% release the median SPX return is not positive or if the average return is less than 0.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both Housing Starts (HOUST) and Retail Sales (RSAFS) are rising month-over-month, it signals synchronized strength in housing and consumption sectors, which together drive broad economic momentum and lead to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year rolling window the average 60-day forward return following months with both HOUST and RSAFS above their previous month's levels is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (t-test p < 0.05), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% indicates a very tight labor market that compresses corporate profit margins via wage inflation, producing downward pressure on equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If following UNRATE readings below 4.5% the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is greater than the overall average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanding economic output that directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any 5-year rolling window the correlation between GDPC1 quarterly change and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is non-positive or if the hit rate of positive returns following positive GDP growth is below 60%, the hypothesis is rejected.
